era: 1981-1982. Hardcore Punk. E=8 -- maximum velocity, Capitol Hill, Alternative Tentacles. THREE-QUARTERS MARK. QUADRANT IV BEGINS.
associated_musicians: Duff McKagan (Ten Minute Warning, degree 265; Guns N' Roses)

theory_nodes.concept: THREE-QUARTERS MARK: 270/360 = 75%. QUADRANT IV BEGINS. The underground before the mainstream occupies the engine's most significant structural milestone in the final quarter.
Duff McKagan trajectory: The Fartz (drums, 1981) → Ten Minute Warning (drums, 1983) → Guns N' Roses (bass, 1985) → 30M+ album sales. Seattle hardcore to global rock.
theory_nodes.description: The most dramatic individual trajectory in the Seattle rock engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Alternative Tentacles: founded by Jello Biafra (Dead Kennedys, 1979). Political hardcore label. The Fartz appearing here = cultural legitimacy + political alignment.
theory_nodes.description: The Biafra imprimatur: the most important political punk label in America.

cross_references.relationship: DUFF McKAGAN. The Fartz → Ten Minute Warning → GN'R. Both Seattle hardcore bands. McKagan in both. Five degrees apart.
Blaine Cook connection. Cook fronted both The Fartz and The Accused at various points. Capitol Hill hardcore continuity.
Seattle hardcore arc continues. Solger (1980) possibly predates The Fartz -- even earlier Seattle hardcore. E=8 continues.
Capitol Hill post-punk. Tom Price connects to Gas Huffer (262). The arc's long-running band.

sps_pair.species: Puget Sound orca S01 pod call
sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 on BOTH SIDES at the THREE-QUARTERS MARK. The Fartz E=8 + Orca S01 E=8. Maximum energy on both tracks simultaneously at 75%.
